---
title: "Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita"
---

## Brief Introduction

This graph shows the relationship between GDP per capita and life expectancy for 142 countries from 1952 to 2007. Each point represents a country-year observation, and colors indicate continents. The x-axis is displayed on a logarithmic scale to better visualize income differences across countries.

```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# A simple global view: scatter of all country-year points, colored by continent
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
for cont, sub in df.groupby("continent"):
    ax.scatter(sub["gdp_per_capita"], sub["life_expectancy"], label=cont, alpha=0.6)

ax.set_xscale("log")
ax.set_xlabel("GDP per capita (log scale)")
ax.set_ylabel("Life expectancy")
ax.set_title("Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita (All Countries, 1952–2007)")
ax.legend()
plt.show()
```

## Interpretation

There is a strong positive relationship between GDP per capita and life expectancy. Countries with higher income levels generally have longer life expectancy. However, the increase in life expectancy slows at higher income levels, suggesting diminishing returns to income. Clear continental patterns are also visible, with Europe and Oceania clustered at higher income and life expectancy levels, while many African countries remain at lower levels.
